claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you enquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children. I'm enquiring about both of them - Alex who is 5 years old, and Blake who is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have two children - Alex (age 5) and Blake (age 10). 

Let me ask you a few questions to determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for both of them.

First question: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Next question: **Do both Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask: **Are you responsible for Alex and Blake's care? By this I mean, are you the one who looks after them and pays for their needs such as food, clothes, and other expenses?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for them. I look after them and pay for everything they need - their food, clothes, school things, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information.

One more important question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for answering all my questions.

Based on the information you've provided, I can now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Let me make my determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

**For Alex (age 5):**
You meet all the eligibility criteria because Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their care and pay for their needs, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

**For Blake (age 10):**
You meet all the eligibility criteria because Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their care and pay for their needs, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for both children. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for their care and pay for their needs (food, clothes, etc.), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

Blake is 10 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for their care and pay for their needs (food, clothes, etc.), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Blake.

The claimant is responsible for both children's care and pays for all their needs including food, clothes, and school supplies. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.
